tooltips.beatmap_path: Path to .osu file to autofill metadata, audio path, and output path, or use as reference
tooltips.title: Song title
tooltips.creator: Beatmap creator/mapper name
tooltips.tags: Tags for searching and categorizing, separated by spaces
tooltips.scroll_speed_ratio: Scroll speed ratio for mania and ctb 0-1
tooltips.lora_path: Path to folder or HuggingFace repository with LoRA weights and config. This can be used to tweak the style of the model beyond its usual capabilities.
tooltips.in_context_options: List of additional context to provide to the model
tooltips.descriptors: List of descriptors for style (1 click = include, 2 clicks = exclude)
tooltips.negative_descriptors: List of negative descriptors for style to avoid (1 click = include, 2 clicks = exclude)

models.v31: Mapperatorinator V31 (Slightly more accurate than V29)

options.export_osz_tooltip: Whether to export a .osz file containing the generated beatmap and audio file
options.add_to_beatmap_tooltip: Whether to merge the generated content with the reference beatmap
options.overwrite_reference_beatmap_tooltip: Whether to overwrite the reference beatmap file instead of creating a new file
options.super_timing_tooltip: Whether to use slow accurate variable BPM timing generator
options.enable_bf16_tooltip: Use BF16 precision for ~15% faster inference on Ampere+ GPUs (RTX 30xx/40xx)

progress.title: Progress
progress.initializing: Initializing process... This may take a moment.
progress.warning_detected: Warning on this job (Will continue to generate)

success.settings_reset: All settings reset to default values

error.start_failed: Failed to start inference process. Check backend console.
error.validation_failed: Error validating paths. Check console for details.
error.audio_or_beatmap_required: Either 'Beatmap Path' or 'Audio Path' are required for running inference
error.output_or_beatmap_required: Either 'Output Path' or 'Beatmap Path' are required for running inference
error.browse_failed: Could not browse. Ensure the backend API is running.
error.connection_lost: Error: Connection to process stream lost.
error.file_not_found: Error: A required file was not found.
error.generation_error: There was an error while creating the beatmap. Check console/logs for details.
error.task_error: There was an error starting or executing the generation task.
error.config_invalid: Please select a valid JSON configuration file.
error.cancel_failed: Failed to send cancel request. Unknown error.
error.open_folder_failed: Failed to open folder via backend.
error.open_log_failed: Failed to open log file via backend.

confirm.reset_settings: Are you sure you want to reset all settings to default values? This cannot be undone.

links.open_folder: Click here to open the folder containing your map.
links.beatmap_created: Click Here to go to the created beatmap...

gimmick.label: gimmick
gimmick.tooltip: Focused on a single unique design or gameplay idea.

2B.label: 2B
2B.tooltip: Includes gameplay elements with two or more objects placed simultaneously.

slider only.label: slider only
slider only.tooltip: Restricts object choice to sliders only.

circle only.label: circle only
circle only.tooltip: Restricts object choice to circles only.

swing.label: swing
swing.tooltip: Uses 1/3, 1/6, and 1/12 snap divisors for most/all objects.

collab.label: collab
collab.tooltip: A map with two or more associated mappers.

megacollab.label: megacollab
megacollab.tooltip: A map with 8 or more associated mappers.

marathon.label: marathon
marathon.tooltip: A map with a drain time of over 5 minutes.

gungathon.label: gungathon
gungathon.tooltip: A map with a drain time of over 10 minutes.

multi-song.label: multi-song
multi-song.tooltip: Contains multiple songs within the audio.

variable timing.label: variable timing
variable timing.tooltip: Contains multiple timing points, usually for non-metronome songs.

time signatures.label: time signatures
time signatures.tooltip: Many changes or uncommon time signatures.

storyboard.label: storyboard
storyboard.tooltip: Contains a storyboard that enhances gameplay experience.

storyboard gimmick.label: storyboard gimmick
storyboard gimmick.tooltip: Uses storyboard elements that change how the map is played.

keysounds.label: keysounds
keysounds.tooltip: Uses various pitched hitsounds to create a melody.

download unavailable.label: download unavailable
download unavailable.tooltip: Cannot be downloaded from the osu! website.

custom skin.label: custom skin
custom skin.tooltip: Utilizes custom skin elements and graphics.

featured artist.label: featured artist
featured artist.tooltip: Features song(s) from osu!'s Featured Artist listing.

custom song.label: custom song
custom song.tooltip: Maps a song made specifically for the map.

geometric.label: geometric
geometric.tooltip: Incorporates geometric shapes within the design.

grid snap.label: grid snap
grid snap.tooltip: Objects are placed along a square grid.

hexgrid.label: hexgrid
hexgrid.tooltip: Objects are placed along a hexagonal grid.

freeform.label: freeform
freeform.tooltip: A loose approach to visual structure.

old-style revival.label: old-style revival
old-style revival.tooltip: Emulates a style from early mapping.

slidershapes.label: slidershapes
slidershapes.tooltip: Uses a variety of slider designs.

distance snapped.label: distance snapped
distance snapped.tooltip: Uses osu's built-in distance snap feature.

iNiS-style.label: iNiS-style
iNiS-style.tooltip: Originates from the original DS games.

high contrast.label: high contrast
high contrast.tooltip: Uses flashy ideas to follow music changes.

improvisation.label: improvisation
improvisation.tooltip: Uses patterns that do not directly match the music.

playfield constraint.label: playfield constraint
playfield constraint.tooltip: Restricts object placement to a part of the playfield.

video gimmick.label: video gimmick
video gimmick.tooltip: References the background video in its patterning.

colorhax.label: colorhax
colorhax.tooltip: Intentional use of combo colors for immersion.

complex sv.label: complex sv
complex sv.tooltip: Large changes in slider velocity to test reading.

visually dense.label: visually dense
visually dense.tooltip: Patterns with many visible notes that make reading hard.

sharp aim.label: sharp aim
sharp aim.tooltip: Heavy use of sharp angle movement.

aim control.label: aim control
aim control.tooltip: Features abrupt velocity or direction changes.

bursts.label: bursts
bursts.tooltip: Continuous alternating patterns, typically 9 notes or less.

streams.label: streams
streams.tooltip: Continuous alternating patterns, typically more than 9 notes.

spaced streams.label: spaced streams
spaced streams.tooltip: Streams with large spacing between notes.

mapping contest.label: mapping contest
mapping contest.tooltip: An entry for a mapping contest.

tournament custom.label: tournament custom
tournament custom.tooltip: A custom map for a tournament.

port.label: port
port.tooltip: Originally created for other media then imported.

tag_1.label: messy
tag_1.tooltip: Visually chaotic and intentionally disorganised patterns, often involving many overlaps and unequal visual spacing between objects.

tag_3.label: chaotic
tag_3.tooltip: Unpredictable map design, often testing unusual skillsets.

tag_4.label: geometric
tag_4.tooltip: Incorporates geometric shapes within the map's visual design.

tag_5.label: freeform
tag_5.tooltip: An unrestrained and loose approach towards visual structure.

tag_6.label: grid snap
tag_6.tooltip: Objects are placed along a square grid, typically using osu!'s in-built grid snap feature.

tag_7.label: hexgrid
tag_7.tooltip: Objects are placed along a hexagonal grid.

tag_8.label: symmetrical
tag_8.tooltip: Employs symmetry within the map design, often mirroring elements along the horizontal centreline.

tag_9.label: playfield constraint
tag_9.tooltip: Restricts object placement to a certain part of the playfield.

tag_10.label: old-style revival
tag_10.tooltip: Emulates a style closely associated with early mapping, typically to pay homage or invoke nostalgia.

tag_11.label: repetition
tag_11.tooltip: Features the use of recognizable identical patterns or gameplay elements.

tag_12.label: progression
tag_12.tooltip: Contains a gradual advancement in difficulty or concept across the map.

tag_13.label: high contrast
tag_13.tooltip: Uses flashy ideas to follow changes in the music, creating stark differences between different sections of the song.

tag_14.label: improvisation
tag_14.tooltip: Uses patterns that do not correspond to sounds in the music, often using hitsounds to add sounds in.

tag_16.label: slider tech
tag_16.tooltip: Tests uncommon skills involving sliders, such as heavy use of kicksliders or aim on complex slidershapes.

tag_17.label: finger control
tag_17.tooltip: Uses complex applications of rhythms in order to test the player's tapping ability.

tag_18.label: complex sv
tag_18.tooltip: Changes slider velocity by large amounts in order to test the player's reading and aim ability.

tag_19.label: jumps
tag_19.tooltip: Focuses heavily on jumps, i.e. circles spaced far apart that require the player to move towards, slow down to hit, then speed up to move towards the next object.

tag_20.label: sharp
tag_20.tooltip: Patterns with heavy use of sharp angle movement.

tag_21.label: wide
tag_21.tooltip: Patterns with heavy use of wide angle movement.

tag_22.label: linear
tag_22.tooltip: Patterns that require the player to move continuously in a straight or nearly straight line.

tag_23.label: aim control
tag_23.tooltip: Patterns with velocity or direction changes which strongly go against a player's natural movement pattern.

tag_24.label: flow aim
tag_24.tooltip: Patterns with fully continuous cursor movement, usually due to a combination of wide angles and little time between objects.

tag_25.label: bursts
tag_25.tooltip: Patterns requiring consecutively tapping 5-9 note groups.

tag_26.label: streams
tag_26.tooltip: Patterns requiring continuous note hits, typically more than 9 notes.

tag_27.label: spaced streams
tag_27.tooltip: Streams with large spacing, typically ones where the notes don't overlap.

tag_28.label: stamina
tag_28.tooltip: Tests a player's ability to tap dense rhythms over long periods of time.

tag_29.label: cutstreams
tag_29.tooltip: Streams in which the spacing of certain notes is much larger than the rest of the stream.

tag_30.label: reading
tag_30.tooltip: Tests a player's reading skill, i.e. patterns that obfuscate note order and/or timing.

tag_31.label: visually dense
tag_31.tooltip: Contains patterns where the amount of visible notes makes determining note order and/or timing difficult.

tag_32.label: overlaps
tag_32.tooltip: Contains patterns where overlapped objects make determining note order and/or timing difficult.

tag_33.label: precision
tag_33.tooltip: Colloquial term for maps which require fine, precise movement to aim correctly. Typically refers to maps with circle sizes above and including 6.

tag_34.label: alt
tag_34.tooltip: Colloquial term for maps which use rhythms that encourage the player to alternate notes. Typically distinct from burst or stream maps.

tag_35.label: collab
tag_35.tooltip: A map with two or more associated mappers.

tag_39.label: variable timing
tag_39.tooltip: Contains multiple timing points, usually required for songs not recorded to a metronome.

tag_40.label: time signatures
tag_40.tooltip: A song featuring many changes in time signature or uses an uncommon time signature.

tag_41.label: clean
tag_41.tooltip: Visually uncluttered and organised patterns, often involving few overlaps and equal visual spacing between objects.

tag_42.label: complex slidershapes
tag_42.tooltip: Uses a large variety of slider designs.

tag_43.label: aspire
tag_43.tooltip: Uses glitches to provide gameplay or visual effects that are otherwise impossible to achieve, originating from the annual Aspire mapping contest.

tag_44.label: distance snap
tag_44.tooltip: Uses osu's in-built distance snap feature for most/all of the map.

tag_45.label: mapping contest
tag_45.tooltip: An entry for a mapping contest.

tag_46.label: tournament custom
tag_46.tooltip: A map created for a playing tournament.

tag_48.label: complex snap
tag_48.tooltip: Maps that feature prominent usage of mixed or unusual snap divisors.

tag_49.label: iNiS-style
tag_49.tooltip: A style originating from the original DS games, recognizable by its emphasis on vocal rhythm, constant slider velocity, and easily understandable grid-snapped patterns.

tag_50.label: 2B
tag_50.tooltip: Includes gameplay elements with two or more objects placed simultaneously. The term originates from a Chinese transliteration of 'idiot'.

tag_51.label: playfield usage
tag_51.tooltip: Includes deliberate use of the playfield within the map design.

tag_52.label: tag
tag_52.tooltip: Includes gameplay designed for the multiplayer tag mode, where multiple players complete a map co-operatively.

tag_53.label: difficulty spike
tag_53.tooltip: Contains a sudden and significant increase in challenge within the map's gameplay.

tag_54.label: avant-garde
tag_54.tooltip: Employs boundary-pushing and experimental philosophies to map design, often foregoing gameplay and aesthetic conventions to extreme measures.

tag_55.label: keysounds
tag_55.tooltip: Contains hitsounds that use various pitched samples to create a melody, typically following one within the song.

tag_56.label: storyboard
tag_56.tooltip: Includes a storyboard that changes how the map is played, usually by changing a map's visuals by using storyboard elements in place of showing the map's hitobjects.

tag_57.label: low sv
tag_57.tooltip: Features prominent low slider velocity usage as a key part of map design.

tag_58.label: high sv
tag_58.tooltip: Features prominent high slider velocity usage as a key part of map design.

tag_59.label: mega collab
tag_59.tooltip: A map with 8 or more associated mappers.

tag_60.label: slider only
tag_60.tooltip: Restricts object choice to sliders only.

tag_61.label: circle only
tag_61.tooltip: Restricts object choice to circles only.

tag_63.label: custom skin
tag_63.tooltip: Utilizes custom skin elements and graphics.

tag_64.label: video
tag_64.tooltip: Employs patterning that closely references the included background video.

tag_66.label: perfect stacks
tag_66.tooltip: Features perfectly overlapped stacked notes using low stack leniency.

tag_67.label: ninja spinners
tag_67.tooltip: Features spinners that are very short in duration.

tag_70.label: accelerating bpm
tag_70.tooltip: A song featuring progressively increasing tempo.

tag_71.label: custom song
tag_71.tooltip: Maps a song made specifically for the map. This includes songs created for a mapping contest which the map participated in.

tag_72.label: mirrored
tag_72.tooltip: A map that features patterns that are mirrored on an axes in quick successions.

tag_73.label: vocal
tag_73.tooltip: Patterning that focuses mainly on vocals.

tag_74.label: tnt
tag_74.tooltip: A map that imitates the mapping style in Taiko No Tatsujin.

tag_75.label: speed
tag_75.tooltip: A map that requires constant tapping at high BPMs.

tag_76.label: double bpm
tag_76.tooltip: A map that plays at double the speed than what the BPM indicates.

tag_77.label: finisher-heavy
tag_77.tooltip: Features finishers used in an unconventional manner or in large amounts.

tag_78.label: mono-heavy
tag_78.tooltip: Features monos used in large amounts.

tag_79.label: reversed
tag_79.tooltip: A map using reversed patterns in a regularly consecutive manner.

tag_80.label: yellow notes
tag_80.tooltip: A map featuring frequent use of extremely short sliders to simulate ghost notes.

tag_82.label: barlines
tag_82.tooltip: A map that makes use of barlines to enhance visuals or replace notes.

tag_83.label: memory
tag_83.tooltip: A map designed around a memorization concept.

tag_84.label: taikosu
tag_84.tooltip: A map designed with both osu! and osu!taiko in mind.

tag_85.label: swing
tag_85.tooltip: A song with prominent use of swing rhythms.

tag_86.label: improvisation
tag_86.tooltip: A map that's based on full improvisation that works as additional layer to the song.

tag_87.label: tech
tag_87.tooltip: A map that frequently makes use of complex snaps.

tag_88.label: convert
tag_88.tooltip: A map that imitates the converted maps from osu!.

tag_89.label: hitsounds
tag_89.tooltip: A map that makes use of non-default hitsound samplesets.

tag_90.label: tech
tag_90.tooltip: A map with the focus on lots of 1/4 sliders, hypersliders and stacks.

tag_91.label: flow
tag_91.tooltip: A map focused on natural and intuitive movement patterns.

tag_92.label: antiflow
tag_92.tooltip: A map focused on strong direction or velocity changes that go against a player's natural movement pattern.

tag_93.label: jump
tag_93.tooltip: A map that focuses on 1/2 constant dashes or hyperdashes.

tag_94.label: no hyperdashes
tag_94.tooltip: A map that doesn’t use hyperdashes even if allowed for its respective difficulty.

tag_95.label: long sliders
tag_95.tooltip: A map that uses sliders for an extended period of time, having regular gameplay around catching juice drops and droplets instead of fruits.

tag_96.label: spinner-heavy
tag_96.tooltip: A map that features heavy usage of spinners.

tag_97.label: convert
tag_97.tooltip: A map that imitates the converted maps from osu!, where the structure and distances are irregular.

tag_98.label: precision
tag_98.tooltip: Colloquial term for maps which require fine, precise movement to catch fruits. Typically refers to maps with circle sizes above and including 6.

tag_99.label: wiggles
tag_99.tooltip: A map with a focus on quick directional changes.

tag_100.label: hyperwalks
tag_100.tooltip: A map which makes use of hyperdashes that require the player to walk, as otherwise, you will overshoot them.

tag_101.label: dodge the beat
tag_101.tooltip: A map where the player needs to avoid every object.

tag_102.label: jumpstream
tag_102.tooltip: Stream with a mix of 2-note sized chords

tag_103.label: handstream
tag_103.tooltip: Stream with a mix of 3-note sized chords

tag_104.label: quadstream
tag_104.tooltip: Stream with a mix of 4-note sized chords

tag_105.label: chordjack
tag_105.tooltip: Maps that feature evenly spaced chords, with several consecutive notes stacked on the same columns.

tag_106.label: longjack
tag_106.tooltip: Maps that feature long chains of consecutive notes on the same column.

tag_107.label: speedjack
tag_107.tooltip: Maps that feature shorter jack sequences, characterized by the faster timeframe between consecutive stacked notes.

tag_108.label: wristjack
tag_108.tooltip: Fast and/or fairly dense chordjack maps, whose optimal playing technique involves the use of the player’s wrist movements.

tag_109.label: chordstream
tag_109.tooltip: Maps that make use of streams with a mix of differently-sized chords

tag_110.label: delay
tag_110.tooltip: Maps that feature high snap streams based on the song’s delayed sound effect.

tag_111.label: tech
tag_111.tooltip: A map that frequently makes use of complex snaps.

tag_112.label: mixed rice
tag_112.tooltip: Maps that make use of multiple rice patterning styles.

tag_113.label: LN coordination
tag_113.tooltip: Maps which require holding multiple long notes simultaneously while hitting other patterns.

tag_114.label: LN release
tag_114.tooltip: Maps that make use of different long note holds ending.

tag_115.label: LN inverse
tag_115.tooltip: Maps that feature long note holds that emphasize constant holds and releases in quick succession. Most distinct feature of Inverse is the “negative spaces” in the patterning.

tag_116.label: LN density
tag_116.tooltip: Maps that feature dense long note streams without breaks.

tag_117.label: LN mixed
tag_117.tooltip: Maps that make use of multiple long note patterning styles.

tag_118.label: generic hybrid
tag_118.tooltip: Maps that feature the combination of both straightforward rice and Long Notes patterning.

tag_119.label: technical hybrid
tag_119.tooltip: Maps that feature the combination of both technical rice and Long Notes patterning.

tag_120.label: tiebreaker
tag_120.tooltip: Maps that contain most of the skill sets from different categories, and are usually longer than 5 minutes.

tag_121.label: o2jam
tag_121.tooltip: Map that mimics traditional mapping techniques usually found in O2jam charts.

tag_122.label: N+1
tag_122.tooltip: A specific type of playstyle where the leftmost column is mapped independently from the rest of the columns, which otherwise form a standard playstyle.

tag_123.label: dump
tag_123.tooltip: Maps that use groups of objects focusing more on the extension and intensity of the sounds, in contrast with using individual notes to follow each's sound timing accurately.

tag_124.label: complex sv
tag_124.tooltip: Changes slider velocity by large amounts in order to test the player's reading ability.

tag_125.label: complex sv
tag_125.tooltip: Changes slider velocity by large amounts in order to test the player's reading ability.

tag_128.label: stamina
tag_128.tooltip: Tests a player's ability to tap dense rhythms over long periods of time.

tag_129.label: stamina
tag_129.tooltip: Tests a player's ability to tap dense rhythms over long periods of time.

tag_132.label: cross-screen
tag_132.tooltip: Frequent use of jumps with a lot of spacing, usually placed on opposite sides of the playfield.

tag_133.label: stamina
tag_133.tooltip: Tests a player's ability to aim spaced jumps over long periods of time.

tag_135.label: complex snap
tag_135.tooltip: Maps that feature prominent usage of mixed or unusual snap divisors.

tag_136.label: complex snap
tag_136.tooltip: Maps that feature prominent usage of mixed or unusual snap divisors.

tag_137.label: gimmick
tag_137.tooltip: Distinct or obscure gameplay elements that cannot be categorised with common skillsets.

tag_139.label: back and forth
tag_139.tooltip: Frequent use of back and forth patterns.

tag_140.label: freeform
tag_140.tooltip: Frequent use of jumps without distinct patterning.

tag_141.label: doubles
tag_141.tooltip: Patterns requiring consecutively tapping 2 note groups.

tag_142.label: quads
tag_142.tooltip: Patterns requiring consecutively tapping 4 note groups.

tag_143.label: inspo
tag_143.tooltip: Maps that draw inspiration directly from other maps/mappers.

tag_144.label: conceptual
tag_144.tooltip: Distinct map design choices that do not follow ordinary mapping conventions, typically to creatively express part of a song.

tag_145.label: combo colours
tag_145.tooltip: Maps that adjust combo colours in conjunction with variations in the song, also referred to as colourhax.
